title: Personalized Greeting
description:
Write a program that simulates asking for a name and age. Store 'Alex' in a variable called name, store 25 in a variable called age. Then print:

Hello Alex, you are 25 years old!

Use variables and an f-string to produce the output.
starter_code: # Store the name and age in variables
expected_output: Hello Alex, you are 25 years old!

code_requirements.description: Creates a variable called 'name' set to 'Alex'
code_requirements.fail_message: Make sure you create a variable called 'name' with the value 'Alex'.

code_requirements.description: Creates a variable called 'age' set to 25
code_requirements.fail_message: Make sure you create a variable called 'age' with the value 25.

code_requirements.description: Uses an f-string to build the output
code_requirements.fail_message: Use an f-string (e.g. f"Hello {name}...") to build the output instead of hardcoding the full sentence.

hints: Create a variable called name with the value 'Alex' and a variable called age with the value 25
Use an f-string to embed both variables: f"Hello {name}, you are {age} years old!"
The comma after Hello and the spaces around 'you' must match exactly

tests.expected_output: Hello Alex, you are 25 years old!
